statistics.tankerTraffic: Day 49 Late: CENTCOM blockade DAY 4. Hormuz summit outcomes: 40 nations plan defensive multinational naval mission — military planning meeting next week. France-UK explicitly reject joining US blockade. Iran Deputy FM at Antalya rejects temporary ceasefire. Ship spoofing detected — Iran-linked tanker 'Alicia' used false AIS. 13-14 ships turned back. 10,000+ US troops, 12+ warships enforcing. USS George H.W. Bush CSG en route as third carrier. Oil surges: Brent $99.39 (+24.5%), WTI $93.74. Gas $4.09. Blockade Day 46.
statistics.note: Day 46 PM (Ceasefire Day 7): CENTCOM BLOCKADE DAY 1 COMPLETE. 6 ships U-turned. No vessels reached Iranian ports. 3 vessels (incl. Chinese-owned Rich Starry, 2 sanctioned tankers) transited strait but NOT to Iranian ports. China: 'Strait is open to us.' 157.7M bbl Iranian oil on water (97.6% China-bound). Saudi + 5 Gulf states demand US end blockade. Houthi counter-blockade threat at Bab al-Mandeb. Oil plunges: Brent $94.87, WTI $91.42 on talk hopes. IEA: March 10.1M bpd lost. Pakistan pushing Islamabad Round 2. Ceasefire expires April 22. Blockade Day 43.
statistics.iranNewDemand: Iran Parliament approved Hormuz toll plan (March 31): rial-denominated tolls, bans US/Israeli vessels + ships from sanctioning countries. Revenue potential ~$600-800M/month. Speaker Ghalibaf warns of US plan to seize Iranian island. Rubio calls toll plan 'illegal, unacceptable, dangerous.'

timeline.event: Iran begins deploying naval mines in Strait of Hormuz
IRGC deploys anti-ship missiles along Iranian coastline
Full blockade declared. War-risk insurance pulled by Lloyd's of London.
4 commercial vessels struck by mines/missiles. 150+ vessels at anchor.
1 oil tanker sunk. All major shipping companies halt Persian Gulf operations.
US Navy minesweeping operations begin. No commercial transit.
Only 9 vessels total crossed strait this week. Dry bulk transits down 91%. ~280 bulkers trapped. IRGC drones hit tankers Prima and Louise P.
Tehran oil depots bombed — fuel distribution halted. Brent crude +27% for the week. US Navy still not escorting. IRGC vows 6+ months capability.

Strait effectively closed — zero transits for 48+ hours. 310+ vessels at anchor. 8 commercial vessels damaged total, 2 sunk. Brent crude hits $119.50 intraday before settling at $103.47. US minesweeping makes minimal progress. IRGC deploys additional fast attack boats.

Day 11 — Only 13 vessels/day average; March 8 saw only 2 outbound transits. 170+ containerships trapped, 150+ tankers at anchor. Insurance withdrawn by all major providers. VLCC charter rates surge from $50K to $400K/day. Brent crude holds above $110.

Day 12 — 3 more ships attacked (total 14 damaged/sunk since war began). Shipping traffic down 95% from pre-war. 330+ vessels at anchor. IEA approves record 400M barrel emergency reserve release. Brent rebounds +3.4% to $90.77. Iran deploys Khoramshahr super-heavy missiles.

Day 13 — Two more tankers hit in Iraqi waters (total 16 damaged/sunk). 280+ bulk carriers stranded. Dry bulk trade down 91%. Brent crosses $100. IEA 400M barrel release across 32 countries (US contributing 172M from SPR) fails to contain surge. Mojtaba Khamenei vows Hormuz stays closed.

No commercial transits in either direction for 24 hours (Bloomberg). Iran considering yuan-only access. 2 Indian LPG carriers allowed under Indian Navy escort — rare exception. Hegseth: 'no clear evidence' of new mines but strait 'won't remain contested.'

Day 14: Trump strikes Kharg Island military targets but spares oil. Iran threatens oil companies with US connections. IRGC hits US Embassy Baghdad. 16+ vessels attacked in strait since war began. Iran considering yuan-only passage proposal.

Day 15: Fujairah port oil operations suspended after drone attack. IRGC threatens UAE cities. 20+ commercial vessels attacked since March 1. Hormuz remains effectively closed. 500+ oil/gas tankers and 500+ container ships stranded.

Iran selectively reopens Hormuz — allows Turkish cargo ship, 2 Indian LNG carriers, Saudi oil tanker. Western vessels still prohibited. 150+ ships anchored outside. US 'not ready' to escort oil ships through (Energy Sec. Wright).
timeline.note: Separate from military events same day — this entry covers selective reopening policy

timeline.event: Day 16: IRGC warns any vessel linked to US/Israel is 'legitimate target,' continues to threaten UAE ports. Turkey and India getting selective passage through strait. Western-flagged vessels still prohibited.

Day 16 PM: Iranian supertanker pushes through Hormuz bound for China. Trump calls for naval coalition — no countries publicly agree. Major carriers still suspended. Selective passage continues for Turkey, India, Saudi ships.
timeline.note: Afternoon update — Iranian supertanker transit and coalition proposal

timeline.event: Iran issues evacuation warnings for specific areas of Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and Doha ports; Fujairah oil loading resumes after Saturday fire; two Indian LPG tankers cross with Iran's permission
Trump urges China and allies to send warships; coalition expected to be announced 'this week' (WSJ). No countries publicly commit yet
Dubai airport fuel tank struck by Iranian drone — flights suspended 7+ hours. Fujairah oil hub struck again, oil loading suspended. 300+ ships stranded in Gulf
EU's Kallas proposes Black Sea grain deal-style Hormuz arrangement via UN. Japan PM rules out sending warships. WSJ: coalition announcement imminent

Zero commercial vessel transits recorded on March 14 — first complete stoppage in history. ~400 vessels stuck in surrounding waters. Only shadow fleet and Chinese-linked ships making sporadic crossings.

Iran selectively allowing passage: Pakistan, India, Turkey confirmed. China, France, Italy negotiating. Fewer than 5 ships/day vs 138 historical average. EU Kallas proposes 'Black Sea model' oil corridor with UN.

Treasury Secretary Bessent says US will allow Iranian oil tankers through Hormuz. WSJ reports US building multinational naval escort coalition. Oil drops 4-6% on the news. Pentagon exploring diplomatic off-ramps.

Day 18: Israel kills Ali Larijani. Bessent Hormuz tanker policy still in effect but no commercial vessels transit. Fujairah oil zone struck for 3rd time. Pakistan tanker transited with Iran permission. 340+ vessels stranded.
Iran sends 11.7M barrels to China via shadow fleet despite blockade. Only Chinese-linked, Pakistani, and Indian vessels getting selective passage. Western commercial shipping remains fully blocked.
Iran strikes UAE Shah gas field (20% of UAE gas supply), Fujairah oil terminal hit 3rd time in 4 days
Kuwait-flagged tanker Gas Al Ahmadiah struck near Fujairah. Iraq begins talks with Iran to negotiate ship passage. South Korea reports 26 vessels and 183 crew stranded.

Day 19: IMO emergency 2-day meeting in London — 40-member council may vote on safe maritime corridor resolution. Only 21 tankers total have transited Hormuz since war began — traffic down 97%. Bulk carriers finding new route through Iranian territorial waters. India attempting to escort 6 vessels through.

Iran launched fresh attacks across Persian Gulf on Gulf neighbors. Some ships spoofing transponders as Chinese-flagged to gain passage. Liberian-flagged bulk carrier broadcast destination as 'CHINA OWNER_ALL CREW' to transit.
IMO talks on safe maritime corridor begin; Iran strikes all 6 GCC energy sites. Brent surges past $119 intraday.

Six Western allies issue joint statement on safe passage; UK sends military planners to CENTCOM. 21 total tanker transits since war began vs 100+/day pre-war. Iran allowing select friendly-nation ships.

7-nation Hormuz coalition formed (UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan, Canada). IMO begins negotiations for humanitarian corridor to free ~20,000 stranded seafarers. Dubai crude hits record $166.80/barrel. QatarEnergy declares force majeure on LNG — 17% capacity offline 3-5 years.
Day 21 (Nowruz): Strait remains closed (Day 18). Israel pounds Tehran. Bessent signals unsanctioning 140M barrels Iranian crude. Kuwait refinery struck. Brent $110.28.

Day 18: Iran developing selective 'vetting system' for Hormuz transit — India, China, Turkey, Pakistan, Malaysia negotiating case-by-case passage. At least 9 ships have transited Iranian territorial waters corridor; one tanker reported paying $2M passage fee. ~8 non-Iranian vessels detected via AIS (nearly double recent days). Iran still effectively blocking Western shipping.

Day 22: Blockade Day 19. Iran refuses to negotiate Hormuz reopening while under attack. Trump calls NATO 'COWARDS' for not sending troops to open strait. DIA assessment: Iran capable of 1–6 month blockade. 21 total tankers have crossed via Iranian territorial waters corridor since war began vs 100+/day pre-war. UK authorizes Diego Garcia for US Hormuz strike operations.

Day 22 Evening: Houthis formally threaten to close Bab el-Mandeb Strait — 'Hour Zero' readiness declared by military official Abed al-Thawr. Shipping giants Maersk, Hapag-Lloyd, CMA CGM already suspended Trans-Suez sailings. A double chokepoint scenario (Hormuz + Bab el-Mandeb) would affect ~30% of global seaborne trade. Iran strikes Dimona and Arad, demonstrating continued missile capability on Day 22. Trump calls NATO 'cowards' for not protecting Hormuz.
Trump issues 48-hour ultimatum: Open Hormuz or face strikes on Iran's power plants. Iran counter-threatens Gulf desalination infrastructure. Deadline: March 23 7:44 PM ET.

Day 23: Trump 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum — deadline Monday March 23 7:44 PM ET (~20 hrs remain). Iran rejects compliance; IRGC releases 'Say goodbye to electricity' Gulf energy targeting map. Iran selectively granting passage: Japan approved (joining China, India, Pakistan); IRGC developing formal vetting/registration system requiring ship ownership and cargo data. At least one tanker paid $2M for transit rights. Iran fires 8th missile salvo at Israel. 40,000+ seafarers stranded. Zero commercial transits.

Day 23 Evening: US drops GBU-72 bunker buster bombs on hardened Iranian anti-ship missile bunkers near strait — CENTCOM Admiral Cooper: 8,000+ targets struck total, 130 vessels destroyed, 'largest naval elimination since WWII.' Trump 48-hr ultimatum (expires March 23 7:44 PM ET) met with full Iranian defiance — IRGC threatens 'complete closure' if power plants struck. Cluster munitions hit 7+ central Israel sites including Tel Aviv (15 wounded). Iran firing on Lebanon territory (first confirmed instance).
NATO Sec-Gen Rutte announces 22 nations joining coalition to reopen Hormuz; Saudi East-West Pipeline bypass to Yanbu Red Sea port declared operational as alternate route
Trump's 48-hour ultimatum expires — deadline Monday evening PDT. IRGC restates complete closure threat if power plants struck. 22 NATO nations mobilizing for Hormuz escort operation.

Trump announces 5-day pause on Iran energy strikes citing 'productive talks' — effectively extending Hormuz deadline to March 28. Iran's Defense Council threatens to mine Persian Gulf if coastline/islands attacked. First Iraqi supertanker (2M barrels) crosses strait with AIS signal off using Iran's permission system. Brent crude falls 10%+ from $112 highs to ~$101 on ceasefire diplomacy hopes.

Supertanker Omega Trader (Mitsui OSK) successfully delivers 2M barrels Iraqi crude to Mumbai via Hormuz-Iranian corridor — first Iraqi oil shipment completing a Hormuz transit since blockade began. AIS signal switched off during transit. Iran developing formal vetting/registration system; at least 9 ships have now used Iranian territorial waters corridor. One tanker reportedly paid $2M for transit rights.

Day 25 of war (Hormuz blockade day 22): Iran operating selective vetting system — allowing 9+ ships through for fees of up to $2M per transit. Japan, India, Pakistan, Iraq, Malaysia, China negotiating bilateral passage. Trump extended Hormuz ultimatum after announcing 5-day energy-strike pause citing 'productive talks'. CENTCOM Adm. Cooper confirms planning weeks-long operation to force full reopening. Status: effectively closed for non-vetted vessels.

Day 26 of war (Hormuz blockade day 23): Iran formally rejects US 15-point peace plan including Hormuz access provision — demands instead international recognition of its 'authority' over the strait as a counter-condition. Iran's UN ambassador said 'non-hostile vessels' may pass with Iranian coordination — conditional passage only, not reopening. Philippines declares national State of Energy Emergency (first country globally) citing 98% Gulf oil dependence. ~2,000 vessels and 20,000 seafarers remain stranded. Japan begins largest-ever 30-day oil reserve release (part of IEA 400M barrel effort). Brent volatile: $104.49 early AM, dropped to ~$99 on Trump peace-talks announcement.

Day 27 of war (Hormuz blockade day 24): IRGC Navy Commander Alireza Tangsiri — the architect of the Hormuz blockade — killed by Israel in Bandar Abbas strike. Iran's shift to 'selective blockade' policy (announced March 25, formally allowing 'non-hostile' ships) continues — Trump revealed Iran let 8-10 tankers through as 'goodwill gesture.' Fewer than 10 AIS transits/day vs 138 pre-war. Brent spiked to $105.85 after Iran definitively rejected US 15-point peace plan. UAE struck by Iranian ballistic missiles — 2 killed in Abu Dhabi by debris. Iran fortifying Kharg Island with mines/MANPADs amid US ground operation planning. Iran Parliament Speaker warns of 'vital infrastructure' attacks on any regional country aiding US Kharg seizure. Spain becomes first European nation approved for Hormuz transit — Iran classifies Spain 'non-hostile' after Spain banned US forces from using its bases for Iran strikes. Malaysia also added. Trump extends energy-strike pause to April 6. Brent surges to $107.99-$109. S&P -1.74% worst day of war.

Day 28 (Hormuz blockade Day 25): IRGC Wave 83 targets all four major US Gulf bases simultaneously (Al-Dhafra UAE, Al-Udeidi Saudi, Ali Al-Salem Kuwait, Sheikh Isa Bahrain). IAF destroys Yazd naval missile and sea mine production facility — IDF says most central site for Iran's naval arms production. Pentagon weighing 10,000 additional ground troops including 82nd Airborne; Kharg Island seizure option remains active. CENTCOM confirms 92% of Iran's large naval ships eliminated. Brent $107.99 (+4.1%), WTI $94.48 (+4.6%). Tangsiri's replacement not yet named — IRGC Navy in transition. Selective blockade continues: Spain, Malaysia, China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan approved for transit; fewer than 10 AIS transits per day vs 138 pre-war.

Day 28 (Hormuz blockade Day 25): Iran turns back 3 Chinese ships (COSCO CSCL Indian Ocean, CSCL Arctic Ocean, Marshall Islands-flagged Lotus Rising) near Larak Island — IRGC confirms strait shut to unauthorized vessels. Brent surges to $112.57/bbl (highest since 2022), WTI briefly crosses $100 for first time this conflict. Macquarie warns $200 oil (40% probability) if Hormuz stays shut through June. Iran accepts UN request for humanitarian aid and agricultural shipping corridor. Only 21 tankers transited since war began vs 100+/day pre-war. 150+ ships anchored outside strait. Trump's extended Hormuz reopening deadline: April 7.

Blockade Day 26: Iran attacks Kuwait Airport radar (significantly damaged), Salalah Port Oman (1 injured, crane destroyed, Maersk suspends 48h), UAE industrial zone (5 injured) — simultaneous 3-nation Gulf strike; Pakistan announces Iran agreed to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships transit (2/day) as part of Islamabad mediation; Houthis fire first missile at Israel from Yemen threatening Bab al-Mandeb closure; Islamabad peace summit opens with Saudi/Turkey/Egypt FMs

Islamabad summit: Iran agrees to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships per day through Hormuz (2 daily rate). First partial opening of blockade in 27 days. Trump extends full-reopening deadline to April 6.

Day 30 (Blockade Day 27): First two Pakistani-flagged ships confirmed to have already transited Hormuz — MV Multan (cargo) and P-Akili (crude, 80M+ litres) en route to Karachi, expected to dock March 31. Pentagon ground ops planning for Kharg Island reported by WaPo — adds new escalation risk to blockade. IRGC strikes Bahrain (Alba) and UAE (EGA) aluminium plants. Guterres at EU Council: 'Hormuz closure causes enormous pain around the world.' Trump April 6 full-reopening deadline remains.

Day 30 PM (Blockade Day 27): Islamabad quadrilateral summit (Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Egypt) concludes — Pakistan pledges to host direct US-Iran talks within days, Rubio-Araghchi meeting possible within 48-72 hours. Trump threatens Kharg Island seizure in FT interview — would effectively end all remaining Iranian oil exports. Iran's NPT withdrawal legislation advancing through parliament. Pakistan 20-ship deal confirmed operational; 2 ships/day transiting with IRGC escort. Total blockade: Blockade Day 27, ~17-18M bbl/day blocked (after pipeline bypass capacity).

Day 31 AM (Blockade Day 28): Trump publishes FT interview claiming 'regime change achieved' and threatens to seize Kharg Island (Iran's main oil terminal, ~90% of exports) and 'obliterate' Iran's power plants/oil wells unless deal reached. Tabriz Petrochemical Company struck overnight. Haifa Bazan Oil Refinery set on fire in Iranian barrage. Brent crude surges to $114.90/bbl. Rubio-Araghchi talks expected 'within days' in Islamabad. Spain closes airspace to US military. Trump April 6 full-reopening deadline remains. Pakistan 20-ship/day deal continuing; 2 ships/day transiting.

Day 31 PM (Blockade Day 28): Trump Truth Social post threatens to 'completely obliterate' all of Iran's electric generating plants, oil wells, Kharg Island, and possibly desalination plants by April 6 unless Hormuz fully reopened — Brent intraday spikes to $116. Rubio tells Al Jazeera Hormuz 'will reopen one way or another.' US Special Ops (Rangers, SEALs) and USS Tripoli (3,500 Marines) arrive in Middle East. Iran's joint military command declares private residences of US/Israeli officials 'legitimate targets.' Pakistan 20-ship/day deal continuing (2/day). Brent settles $112.78; WTI first close above $100 since July 2022.

Day 31 late (Blockade Day 28): IRGC drone strikes Kuwaiti VLCC 'Al-Salmi' at anchor in Dubai port area — fire on hull, 24 crew evacuated, oil spill risk. First Iranian strike on commercial vessel in port. IRGC claims Israeli-linked flag. 3 Chinese-flagged vessels transit Hormuz unopposed, underscoring selective blockade. Total crossings since March 1: ~195 (down 95% from peacetime). 2,142 vessels stranded/anchored in Gulf per Bloomberg. Iran charges $2M per transit for approved vessels. Saudi Arabia intercepts 5 Iranian missiles targeting Eastern Province oil infrastructure. Brent surges to $114.90. IMF: 'largest oil disruption in history.' Trump April 6 deadline remains.

Day 32 early (Blockade Day 29): WSJ reports Trump willing to end war without reopening Hormuz — would leave complex reopening for later date. Brent dips ~1% to $111.55 on report. Pakistan FM Dar in Beijing — China's Wang Yi backs mediation. Gulf states (Saudi, UAE, Kuwait, Bahrain) privately told White House they want war to continue until regime change. Iran parliament advancing Hormuz toll legislation. Trump April 6 full-reopening deadline in 6 days.

Iranian drone strikes Kuwaiti VLCC Al-Salmi at Dubai port anchorage — 2M barrels crude, fire contained, 24 crew safe. Iran Parliament approves Hormuz toll plan — bans US/Israeli ships. Rubio: Hormuz will reopen 'one way or another.'

Iranian cruise missile hits QatarEnergy tanker Aqua 1 (17nm N of Ras Laffan); Qatar intercepted 2 of 3 missiles; 21 crew evacuated. Iranian drone hits Kuwait airport fuel depots — massive fire. 1 killed Fujairah (UAE) from drone debris. Bahrain fire. Trump willing to leave without reopening Hormuz per WSJ.

Day 33 (Blockade Day 30): Hormuz transits collapsed to 6 ships (from 130/day pre-war, down 95%). Kharazi struck in Tehran. Pezeshkian open letter. DOE 10M barrel SPR exchange RFP. IEA Birol: 'April much worse than March.' 35-nation Hormuz meeting tomorrow (hosted by Britain). EU extends sanctions. Gas $4.06. Iran Parliament toll plan advancing. IRGC threatens 18 US tech companies.

Day 34 (Blockade Day 31): UK-led 40-nation Hormuz conference concludes (US absent) — signed statement demanding Iran reopen strait, pledged follow-up military planning. Iran-Oman nearly finalize draft for new Hormuz navigation regime — signals Iran preparing post-war framework. Brent surged to $109.35 (+8.1%). Trump set April 6 deadline to reopen or face Kharg Island destruction. Only 6 ships transited April 1 (down 95% from 130/day pre-war). ~2,142 vessels stranded. 27 commercial ships attacked/incidents since March 1. ~207 total crossings since blockade began. OPEC+ meets April 5.

Day 35 (Blockade Day 32): Iran claims shootdown of US F-15E near Qeshm Island — CENTCOM denies. Philippine-flagged vessels now exempted (added April 2). Kuwait Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery hit by drones for 3rd time; desalination plant also struck. ~2,150 vessels stranded. GCC UN resolution vote scheduled Friday April 4. Brent $109.03; WTI $111.54 (WTI-Brent inversion). April 6 Trump deadline 3 days away.

UNSC vote on GCC Hormuz resolution delayed to April 5. UK convenes 40+ countries on Hormuz (without US). China opposes force authorization. 3 ships used new Oman coastline route. Philippines added to Iran's allowed-passage list.

Day 36 (Blockade Day 33): Iran approves limited passage for 'essential goods' ships through Hormuz — oil/gas blockade remains. IRGC claims drone attack on MSC Ishyka container ship (unverified). UNSC vote delayed to next week (Good Friday). Trump 48h ultimatum: April 6 deadline. 5 EU nations demand windfall profit tax.

Day 37 (Blockade Day 34): Oman-Iran deputy FM talks exchange Hormuz transit proposals — first direct bilateral negotiation on passage. 3 Omani ships transit outside Iran's approved corridor. French CMA CGM container ship first Western vessel transit since war began (April 3). Iran toll plan operational — ships paying in yuan/crypto. Trump Easter 'Power Plant Day' threat — April 7 8PM ET deadline. Iran rejects: 'gates of hell.' Markets closed Easter.

Day 38 (Blockade Day 35): Iraq exemption — Iranian military declares Iraq 'exempt from any restrictions.' Iraqi Suezmax tanker 'Ocean Thunder' (~1M bbl Basrah Heavy) transits to Malaysia. Qatar LNG tankers Al Daayen + Rasheeda attempt first Hormuz exit since war began — Al Daayen at 8.8kn toward China, Rasheeda pulled back. Weekly transits up to 53 (from 36). Iran toll plan operational — Parliament approved rial-denominated tolls. 45-day ceasefire proposal from Egypt/Pakistan/Turkey — Iran rejects temporary Hormuz opening. Trump Tuesday 8PM ET deadline TOMORROW.

Day 38: Trump threatens total destruction of Iran tomorrow if Hormuz not reopened. Iran rejects 45-day ceasefire — demands permanent end + compensation. OPEC+ symbolic 206k bpd hike. Only 12 vessels transited Apr 3. Iraqi tanker exemption active. Qatar LNG tankers attempting exit.

Day 38 Night (Blockade Day 35): Iran adviser Velayati threatens 'Resistance front views Bab al-Mandeb as it does Hormuz' — if both straits closed, ~25% of global energy supply blocked. JPMorgan: oil could hit $120-$150/barrel. VP Vance in overnight talks with Pakistan FM + Iran FM on Islamabad Accord ceasefire. Brent surges to $111.25 (+39.3%). Weekly transits at 53 (up from 36). April 7 deadline 8PM ET TOMORROW.

Day 39 (Blockade Day 36): DEADLINE DAY — Trump's 8pm ET ultimatum expires tonight. Hegseth vows 'most intense day of strikes' on Iran. Iran rejects 45-day ceasefire — demands permanent end to war + guarantees + compensation before Hormuz reopens. No active diplomatic channel. Mojtaba Khamenei missing 30 days. Brent $110.05 (+37.8%); WTI $113.42 (+50.2%). Gas ~$4.12.

DEADLINE DAY: Trump 8PM ET deadline for Iran to reopen Hormuz. US strikes 50+ military targets on Kharg Island. Weekly transits reached 53 (highest since blockade). Qatar LNG aborted exit. OPEC+ agreed +206K bpd May.

Day 39 Evening (Blockade Day 35): Russia & China veto UNSC Hormuz resolution (11-2). Iran severs US diplomacy. IRGC launches 11 BMs + 18 drones at Saudi Jubail petrochemical complex — debris ignites fire. King Fahd Causeway closed. Iran's 10-point peace plan rejected by Trump. 8PM ET deadline imminent. Brent $113.40.

Day 40 (Blockade Day 36): CEASEFIRE — Iran SNSC accepts two-week ceasefire. FM Araghchi: 'Safe passage via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces.' Conditional reopening — NOT a full reopening. 20 transits/day (up from 6, still vs 138 pre-war). Oil crashed 16-19% in after-hours, rebounded. Islamabad talks April 10.

CEASEFIRE DAY 1: First ships transit Hormuz since ceasefire. Greek NJ Earth (08:44 UTC) and Liberian Daytona Beach (06:59 UTC) pass through. Only 7 ships exit, 3 enter. 800+ vessels still trapped. Iran demands Armed Forces coordination. Shipowners cautious, assessing safety.

Ceasefire Day 2 AM: Only 3 ships transited Apr 8. IRGC claims shipping halted again after Israel's Lebanon strikes. 800+ vessels remain trapped. Iran warns unauthorized ships 'will be destroyed.' White House calls closure 'completely unacceptable.'

Ceasefire Day 2 midday (Blockade Day 38): IRGC publishes mine map via ISNA/Tasnim showing 'danger zone' over Traffic Separation Scheme. Maham-3 and Maham-7 mines identified. Alternative routes: north of Larak Island (entry) and south of Larak (exit). Press TV reports strait 'fully closed' — tanker AUROURA turned 180°. Maersk: 'ceasefire may create transit opportunities but does not yet provide full maritime certainty.' Oil rebounds: Brent $97.80, WTI $97.53.
Strait remains effectively closed despite ceasefire. IRGC mine map published confirming Maham-3 and Maham-7 mines. Zero oil tankers transit. 800+ vessels trapped.

Day 42 EVE (Blockade Day 39): Only 6 vessels transited (down from 7). 600+ vessels stranded including 325 tankers (230 loaded). Russian-flagged tanker made it through. UAE oil CEO Al Jaber: 'Hormuz is not open.' Iran charging $1M+ per transit. Mine clearance estimated 6-10 weeks. War-risk premiums 0.35-0.45%.

Day 43 AM (Blockade Day 40): US officials reveal Iran CANNOT FIND its own mines — drifted from original positions. Iran releases navigational maps with alt corridors (first official mine acknowledgment). Clearance est. 6-10 weeks. 9 ships transited Thursday (up from 6) but ZERO oil tankers since ceasefire. 800+ freighters stranded. Iran demanding IRGC permission + $1M+ tolls.

Day 43 PM: BREAKING — USS Frank E. Peterson (DDG-121) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) transit Strait of Hormuz east-to-west and back — FIRST US warships since war began Feb 28. CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper begins mine clearance mission. Underwater drones to join in coming days. Iran calls it ceasefire violation, threatens to attack ships 'within 30 minutes.' Iran planted 2,000-6,000 mines but CAN'T LOCATE them all — realistic clearance timeline: 8-14 weeks. Historic Islamabad talks begin in parallel.

Day 44 PM (Blockade Day 41): 3 VLCCs (Serifos, Cospearl Lake, He Rong Hai) exit Hormuz — BIGGEST day of oil movement since war began (~6M barrels). Cospearl Lake laden with Iraqi oil bound for Zhoushan, China. Serifos + He Rong Hai loaded Saudi cargo. Northerly route via Qeshm/Larak Islands. BUT 2 empty supertankers (Agios Fanourios I, Shalamar) U-turned after Islamabad talks collapsed. 230+ loaded tankers still trapped. 800+ freighters stranded. IRGC 'last warning' still in effect. Mine clearance continues — 8-14 weeks.

Day 45 (Blockade Day 42): CENTCOM officially begins enforcing US NAVAL BLOCKADE of ALL maritime traffic entering/exiting Iranian ports at 10:00 AM ET. USS Gerald R. Ford and USS Abraham Lincoln CSGs with 6 guided-missile destroyers. Non-Iranian port traffic through Hormuz continues freely. IRGC warns military vessels approaching = ceasefire violation, 'deadly vortex.' Oil surges: Brent $102.29 (+7.4%), WTI $104.56 (+9.5%). 2,800 ships stranded, 10 crew dead, 20K seafarers. Mine clearance by USS Peterson/Murphy continues.

Day 48 (Blockade Day 45 / CENTCOM Blockade Day 3): Blockade 'fully implemented' — 9-10 tankers turned back, zero breaches. USS Spruance intercepts Iranian-flagged cargo vessel fleeing Bandar Abbas. Iran's Maj. Gen. Abdollahi threatens to block ALL shipping in Persian Gulf, Sea of Oman, AND Red Sea — major escalation. Treasury 'Economic Fury' sanctions 24+ entities in Shamkhani oil smuggling network. Sanctions waiver expires April 19 — will NOT be renewed. Iran NPC suspends all petrochemical exports. Oil eases: Brent $94.89, WTI $91.50. Some sanctioned tankers (Alicia) transited strait to non-Iranian destinations.

Day 49 (Blockade Day 46 / CENTCOM Blockade Day 4): MAJOR — Iran FM Araghchi declares Strait of Hormuz 'completely open' for all commercial vessels during ceasefire period. Status changed to 'partially_blocked' — Iran opened commercial transit but US CENTCOM blockade of Iranian ports continues. 13 ships total turned back by US blockade. Brent settles $90.38 (-9% on day), WTI $83.85 — biggest crash since April 8. Macron-Starmer Paris summit: ~30 countries plan UK-led 'Strictly Defensive' multinational naval mission. Maersk 'cautiously welcomed' reopening but needs guarantees. Insurance still 5x normal (~5% of ship value). Traffic still minimal (~6 vessels/day, zero oil tankers). Trump calls NATO allies 'useless' — 'STAY AWAY.' Trump PROHIBITS Israel from bombing Lebanon. $20B cash-for-uranium deal under discussion (Axios). S&P record 7,126. Nasdaq 13-day streak.

impactedGoods.currentFlow: ~3.8M bbl/day (down 80%+ from pre-war 20M+). Iran declared Hormuz open but shipping industry cautious.
impactedGoods.detail: 20% of global supply cut off — Day 46 of blockade. Iran declared Hormuz 'completely open' but Maersk needs guarantees. Insurance 5x normal. Traffic ~6 vessels/day (zero oil tankers). CENTCOM blockade of Iranian ports continues. Reuters: $50B worth of oil lost in 50 days. 2,800 ships stranded, 10 crew dead, 20K seafarers.

Qatar's LNG facilities damaged. 2 Qatari LNG tankers (Al Daayen + Rasheeda) attempting first Hormuz exit since war began (April 6). Al Daayen toward China with 149,613 cbm LNG. Would be first laden LNG transit since blockade. European gas prices at record levels.

Jet fuel, diesel, gasoline exports halted. US gas $4.166/gal (AAA Day 44 PM) — above $4 since March 31. California: ~$5.93/gal. Diesel: $5.643/gal. Iraq exemption active. OPEC+ +206k bpd for May. March CPI: gasoline +21.2% MoM (largest since 1967). Consumer sentiment 47.6 (record low). Islamabad talks collapsed — no deal path.
Fertilizer and plastics supply chain severely disrupted. Food commodity prices surging 30-50% above pre-war levels.
